title: Il-Mergħa
author: Placeholder
text_mt:
Fuq l-għoljiet tal-ġebel
fejn ir-riħ jitkellem bil-mod,
il-merħla timxi bħal xmara—
wieħed jaqbad, wieħed jibqa' lura.

Ir-ragħaj jaf il-qalb tal-art,
kull ħaġra, kull xewka, kull triq.
Fil-kwiet ta' filgħodu kmieni,
hija u l-klieb—familja waħda.
text_en:
On the hills of stone
where the wind speaks softly,
the flock moves like a river—
one follows, one lingers.

The shepherd knows the heart of the land,
every stone, every thorn, every path.
In the quiet of early morning,
he and the dogs — one family.

title: Il-Ħitan
author: Placeholder
text_mt:
Il-ħitan tas-sejjieħ ma jgħidux xejn,
iżda jżommu kollox f'postu.
Bnew bihom in-nannin tagħna—
bl-idejn, bil-għaraq, bis-sabar.

Aħna wkoll bħalhom:
nibnu, inħarsu, inħobbu.
text_en:
The dry-stone walls say nothing,
but they hold everything in place.
Our grandparents built them—
with hands, with sweat, with patience.

We too are like them:
we build, we guard, we love.

title: Filgħaxija
author: Placeholder
text_mt:
Meta x-xemx tinżel wara l-baħar
u d-dell jitwal fuq il-blat,
il-merħla tieqaf weħidha—
bħallikieku taf li wasal il-mistrieħ.

Il-kelb jinxteħet fil-ħaxix,
ir-ragħaj jibqa' bilwieqfa,
hares lejn id-dawl tal-aħħar
qabel id-dlam jibla' kollox.
text_en:
When the sun sets behind the sea
and shadows stretch across the rock,
the flock stops on its own—
as if it knows that rest has come.

The dog lies in the grass,
the shepherd stands still,
watching the last light
before the dark swallows everything.

title: Il-Ħaruf
author: Placeholder
text_mt:
Insibu l-ħaruf waħdu fil-wied,
qiegħed jibki, ċkejken u mitluf.
Il-kelb jdur bih bil-għaqal,
ir-ragħaj jerfgħu f'idejh.

Il-merħla tilqa'h lura—
mingħajr kliem, mingħajr storbju.
Hekk hi n-natura: taf tħobb
qabel ma taf titkellem.
text_en:
We find the lamb alone in the valley,
crying, small and lost.
The dog circles it gently,
the shepherd lifts it in his hands.

The flock welcomes it back—
without words, without fuss.
This is nature: it knows how to love
before it knows how to speak.
